title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. diaphana
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. diaphana, also known as the fragile fern, is a rare and delicate species of fern that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Cystopteris fragilis subsp. diaphana include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and preventing the growth of certain microorganisms. Herbal preparations of this plant have been traditionally used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and poultices, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential applications.
